channel.title: Home on Andrey Kumanyaev
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Andrey Kumanyaev

item.title: Retrieval got a lot smarter
item.description: How Gortex turned code retrieval from tuned-by-feel into measured-and-ranked — graph-centrality reranking, per-query lexical/semantic blending, query understanding, and a real P@K/R@K/MRR evaluation harness.

item.title: smart_context: less, but more relevant
item.description: smart_context packs the smallest correct working set for a task: dependency-closure selection, a budget that scales with the graph, fidelity tiers with compress_bodies, and delta packing that re-sends only what changed.

item.title: Cross-language resolution & framework awareness
item.description: A code graph is only as good as its edges. A framework dynamic-dispatch synthesizer engine recovers the cross-language and cross-framework call edges that static parsing alone misses.

item.title: Many more languages — and file types
item.description: Gortex now ingests infrastructure-as-code, legacy systems, docs, images, PDFs, and live database schemas into one graph — plus two config-driven SPIs to add your own formats without a fork.

item.title: Deeper analysis
item.description: Push hard codebase questions into the graph: an interprocedural bottleneck analyzer for hidden hot paths that compound across calls, and first-class capability edges that make a least-privilege audit a one-hop query.

item.title: A much better teammate for coding agents
item.description: Two memory layers, a multi-agent coordination registry, and a preview-and-verify edit gate make Gortex a safe, productive teammate for autonomous coding agents over MCP.

item.title: LLM providers & graph-aware routing
item.description: Make the model a configuration choice, not a code dependency: one provider interface, nine interchangeable LLM backends, and graph-aware routing by task complexity.

item.title: A rebuilt storage & performance layer
item.description: A rebuilt storage layer — a pure-Go SQLite backend by default (no CGO, FTS5 search), sidecars that keep agent knowledge off the hot path, warm-restart reconcile in seconds, and graph walks pushed into indexed Store capabilities.

item.title: Multi-workspace & git worktrees
item.description: Gortex tracks each git worktree as an independent repository instance with its own graph, so usages, callers, and resolution stay correctly isolated per checkout.

item.title: Plain-English guide: what changed, and how to use it
item.description: A plain-English tour of the May–June 2026 Gortex updates for the engineers who use it: what each change means in practice, plus the exact commands and settings to get the benefit — no graph theory required.

item.title: Anthropic's large codebase guide is a Gortex tutorial in disguise
item.description: Anthropic published a best-practices guide for Claude Code in large codebases. It's a good guide. It also describes, step by step, exactly what Gortex ships out of the box — and reveals the real cost of the grep-based approach it defends.

item.title: Code intelligence tools compared — the missing row
item.description: A response to rywalker.com/research/code-intelligence-tools, which surveys the code-intelligence landscape and names GitNexus the Tier-1 leader. The article is solid. It also doesn't evaluate Gortex, which directly contradicts three of its headline findings.

item.title: Gortex: Week 2 — DI graph, 3D web UI, dependency graph, enrichment, SQL, and 50 MCP tools
item.description: Two weeks of shipping since the last post: DI framework support (NestJS/FastAPI/Spring/Laravel/Rails), react-three-fiber 3D web UI, gortex eval CLI, external package graph, gortex enrich blame+coverage, six new analyzers, deep Go graph, SQL migration analysis, and feature flags.

item.title: GCX1 — a compact wire format for MCP tool responses
item.description: Median −27.4% tiktoken savings vs JSON, 100% round-trip integrity, across 20 representative MCP tool responses. Shipped, stable, reference decoders in Go and TypeScript.

item.title: Gortex: What Changed and How It Compares to Alternatives
item.description: A follow-up on Gortex after a week of heavy development: 92 languages, daemon mode, GCX1 wire format, LSP edge tiers, cumulative cost tracking — plus an honest comparison with repomix, Cursor's built-in indexing, GitNexus, and Sourcegraph.

item.title: From GitNexus to Gortex: Building a Go Code Intelligence Engine
item.description: How I experimented with GitNexus, discovered its limitations, and built Gortex — a Go code intelligence engine with 48 MCP tools, 33-language support, inline symbol editing, API contract detection, multi-repo mode, and measurable 94%+ token reduction for AI agents.

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.description: Privacy policy for zzet.org — how visitor data is used.

item.title: The Code Citadel - The City Watch
The Code Citadel - The Great Fire
The Code Citadel - The Merchant's Ledger
The Code Citadel - Roads of Mud and Stone
The Code Citadel - The Fall of the Keep
RFC - what did it mean in the Keep through the time
Project configuration. Part 1. Railsclub intro.
About Undev Gitlab fork
Unix shell initialization
About
Search
